faqs.question: Are you licensed and insured?
faqs.answer: Yes. Happy Place Carpentry is an Oregon-licensed contractor (CCB# 254240) and fully insured. We're happy to provide credentials before any work begins.

faqs.question: What areas do you serve?
faqs.answer: We work across Benton, Linn, Marion, and Polk Counties — including Corvallis, Albany, Salem, Lebanon, Philomath, Dallas, and surrounding communities.

faqs.question: How do estimates work?
faqs.answer: Use the free estimate wizard: pick a service, upload a few photos, answer a couple of quick questions, and share your contact info. We follow up to schedule a walk-through and give you a clear written estimate.

faqs.question: Do you handle permits?
faqs.answer: Yes. For decks, fences, and structural work we handle the permit process and inspections so you don't have to.

faqs.question: What materials do you work with?
faqs.answer: Cedar, pressure-treated, and composite decking; wood, vinyl, and metal fencing; and standard cabinetry and trim materials. We'll recommend what fits your budget and the Oregon climate.

faqs.question: How long does a typical project take?
faqs.answer: It depends on scope — a fence might be a few days, a deck one to two weeks, a full kitchen remodel several weeks. We give you a realistic timeline in the written estimate.

faqs.question: Do you offer a warranty?
faqs.answer: Yes. We stand behind our work with a workmanship warranty and honor manufacturer warranties on materials.

faqs.question: Can I supply my own materials?
faqs.answer: In most cases yes — we're glad to work with materials you've chosen, as long as they're suitable for the project and climate.
